channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Former Project Blackbird Developer Forms Eighth Lantern Studios
item.content:encoded:
When Project Blackbird was canceled by Microsoft last year, it left a lot of developers, artists, and others looking for work. While some have found work at other studios, one former developer has decided to start his own.

From the ashes of one cancelation and layoff comes Eighth Lantern Studios, a company founded by former lead encounter designer at Zenimax, Gabe Sheets.

Eighth Lantern began as a side project last year and consists of Sheets as well as several industry veterans from games like Perfect Dark, Remnant II, The Elder Scrolls Online, Call of Duty, and more.

  • More News: EXCLUSIVE: Veteran Studio Working On New Lord of the Rings Game

“At Eighth Lantern Studios, our mission is to combine our AAA experience with fresh ideas to make games that give players a reason to laugh and just have fun with their friends,” said Sheets, a 20-year veteran in the industry. “So, kinda like a finely-tuned gameplay whoopee cushion: timeless, endlessly replayable, and fun for all ages.”

At the start, the team began working on a prototype for a game while continuing to look for work. Sheets said that the belief was they would test the waters if they felt the prototype had a chance.

“Well, it turns out we all fell in love with the project, and not only that, we’re certain that it’s something worth sharing with the rest of the world,” he said.

Currently, Eighth Lantern is bootstrapping its first game, a third-person roguelite puppet shooter. You can learn more about the studio at its website and on social media.

What do you think of former AAA developers branching out and starting their own studios? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read more exclusive details about the recent Eidos Montreal layoffs and what it means for the studio. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Dave Chappelle is Coming to Black Ops 7 in Season 3
item.content:encoded:
Black Ops 7 Season 3 store highlights have been revealed, and players can soon grab Dave Chappelle as an operator.

Ahead of Black Ops 7 Season 3’s arrival on April 2, 2026, the developers have revealed a lot of the new content players should expect in the new update, including new multiplayer maps, modes, Zombies map, new Warzone POI, and more. Now, the devs have revealed the battle pass and store content.

Black Ops 7 Is Getting A Dave Chappelle Operator

As a part of the new store content update for Black Ops 7 Season 3, players are getting the Half-Baked Tracer Pack, based on the popular 1998 comedy starring Dave Chappelle. The pack includes a new operator with two skins inspired by the comedian’s character in the movie. Moreover, players receive new blueprints, a brand-new finishing move, and much more. Players can check all that is included in this BO7 Tracer Pack below:

  • New Operator with two New “Thurgood” inspired Operator Skins
  • “Custodial” M15 Mod 0 Assault Rifle Blueprint (Tracers, Death FX)
  • “Smoke ‘Em Dead” VST SMG Blueprint (New Weapon, Tracers, Death FX)
  • “Snip-Snap-Sack” Hawker HX Sniper Rifle Blueprint (Tracers, Death FX)
  • “Fish Food” Finishing Move
  • “Smoking Makes You Fly” Emote
  • “Pouch of Something” Weapon Charm
  • “Lickity Clean” Animated Calling Card
  • “Trippin’ Green” Animated Emblem
  • “One and the Same” Loading Screen

In other news, Call of Duty: Black Ops 7’s Design Director has acknowledged comments about alleged skill-based damage concerns in the game. Additionally, FaZe Vegas has won the Call of Duty League Major 2 tournament at DreamHack Birmingham. What are your thoughts about Dave Chappelle coming to Black Ops 7? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Gray Zone Warfare’s new update, helping the game reach its highest player count since launch. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Gen.G Ruler Under Investigation by LCK Following Tax Avoidance Ruling
item.content:encoded:
Gen.G ADC Park ‘Ruler’ Jae-hyuk is in hot water after a 2024 court ruling of tax avoidance against the League of Legends pro was made public.

South Korea’s tax authorities ruled that Ruler must pay additional sums of tax following irregularities involving the LoL player’s father. Ruler expensed significant wages paid to his father for acting as his personal manager, but these expenses were deemed “unnecessary” and non-business related.

The LCK is currently investigating after Ruler was met with major backlash as Korean society has a heavy stigma against tax controversies.

Reports that Ruler could be permanently banned from the LCK appear to be false and due to a bad AI article recap, but the pro could face a maximum suspension or a fine of up to 100 million won ($66,295) according to the league’s rules.

It has been a bad couple of weeks for controversies surrounding LoL players, as the LPL’s Naiyauo was permanently banned for match-fixing.

Ruler’s Tax Dodging Allegations

Ruler was judged to have intentionally dodged taxes through two incidents involving his father.

Early in his career, Ruler paid his father a significant amount of “labour costs” between 2018 and 2021, employing his dad as a personal manager while deducting these payments as business expenses.

Tax authorities rejected this expense claim, finding inconclusive evidence that his father had performed managerial duties. The court ruling also stated:

“A professional player who has been under an exclusive contract with his team since 2016 and has remained active within that structure has no reason to engage an external manager or incur additional costs, as the team exclusively manages all of his activities and bears the associated expenses.”

Ruler’s representatives appealed this, arguing that the player needed managerial services, such as negotiating salary and handling interview terms, which had been handled by his father before the ADC signed with an agency in 2022. This argument was rejected.

Additionally, it was found that Ruler had invested in stocks under his father’s name in a title trust arrangement. While it was argued that this was to allow Ruler’s father to manage the investment on his behalf, this effectively allowed the family to avoid higher dividend tax brackets.

Ruler’s representatives argued this had been a name declaration error and had only resulted in a minor tax reduction, but this was also denied.

Ruler now could face a potential suspension, which would be troubling for Gen.G as the LoL esports Spring Split is starting now.

Would a suspension be justified or is a fine from the LCK enough? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP
